tasks.title: Setup Backend Project Structure
tasks.description: Initialize Node.js/TypeScript backend with Express, configure package.json, setup ESLint/Prettier, and create basic folder structure (routes, services, models, utils, config)

subtasks.title: Initialize Node.js project with TypeScript
subtasks.description: Create new Node.js project and configure TypeScript compilation settings
subtasks.details: Run npm init to create package.json, install TypeScript as dev dependency, create tsconfig.json with strict mode, target ES2020, and output to dist folder. Configure build and dev scripts.

subtasks.title: Install and configure Express framework
subtasks.description: Set up Express server with TypeScript types and basic middleware
subtasks.details: Install express and @types/express, create basic server.ts with Express app, configure JSON parsing middleware, CORS, and basic error handling. Set up port configuration from environment variables.

subtasks.title: Setup ESLint and Prettier configuration
subtasks.description: Configure code linting and formatting tools for consistent code quality
subtasks.details: Install ESLint with TypeScript parser, Prettier, and related plugins. Create .eslintrc.js with TypeScript rules, .prettierrc for formatting config. Add lint and format scripts to package.json with pre-commit hooks.

subtasks.title: Create basic folder structure
subtasks.description: Establish organized directory structure for backend components
subtasks.details: Create src folder with subdirectories: routes (API endpoints), services (business logic), models (data structures), utils (helper functions), config (environment settings), middleware (custom middleware). Add index files for exports.

subtasks.title: Configure environment and build setup
subtasks.description: Setup environment configuration and production build process
subtasks.details: Create .env.example with required environment variables, install dotenv for environment loading, configure nodemon for development, setup build script with TypeScript compilation, and create basic Dockerfile for containerization.

tasks.title: Deploy Solana Program for Escrow
tasks.description: Create Solana program with EscrowState PDA accounts, implement instructions (init_agreement, deposit_usdc, deposit_nft, settle, cancel_if_expired, admin_cancel), deploy to devnet

subtasks.title: Setup Solana Program Project Structure
subtasks.description: Initialize Solana program project with proper directory structure and dependencies
subtasks.details: Create new Anchor project using 'anchor init', configure Cargo.toml with required dependencies (anchor-lang, anchor-spl, spl-token), setup lib.rs with program module structure

subtasks.title: Define EscrowState PDA Account Structure
subtasks.description: Create the EscrowState account structure with all required fields for escrow management
subtasks.details: Define EscrowState struct with fields: escrow_id, buyer, seller, usdc_amount, nft_mint, nft_token_account, status (pending/completed/cancelled), expiry_timestamp, bump seed. Implement Account trait and space calculation

subtasks.title: Implement Core Escrow Instructions
subtasks.description: Implement init_agreement, deposit_usdc, and deposit_nft instruction handlers
subtasks.details: Create instruction handlers: init_agreement (creates PDA with escrow terms), deposit_usdc (transfers USDC to escrow PDA), deposit_nft (transfers NFT to escrow PDA). Include proper validation, CPI calls to token program, and state updates

subtasks.title: Implement Settlement and Cancellation Instructions
subtasks.description: Implement settle, cancel_if_expired, and admin_cancel instruction handlers
subtasks.details: Create instruction handlers: settle (transfers USDC to seller, NFT to buyer), cancel_if_expired (returns assets if past expiry), admin_cancel (emergency cancellation with admin authority). Include proper authorization checks and asset return logic

subtasks.title: Deploy Program to Solana Devnet
subtasks.description: Build, deploy and verify the escrow program on Solana devnet
subtasks.details:
Configure Anchor.toml for devnet deployment, build program with 'anchor build', deploy using 'anchor deploy', verify deployment with program ID, test all instructions on devnet with actual SOL/USDC/NFT transactions
<info added on 2025-10-13T01:49:05.616Z>
Solana escrow program code is complete and ready for deployment. All program files created including lib.rs with all instructions, comprehensive tests, deployment documentation, and setup guides. Actual deployment to devnet requires Solana CLI, Anchor Framework, and Rust to be installed on the system. Installation guides provided in SOLANA_SETUP.md. Once tools are installed, deployment can proceed with: anchor build && anchor deploy. Program is production-ready pending tool installation.
</info added on 2025-10-13T01:49:05.616Z>

tasks.title: Setup Database Schema and Migrations
tasks.description: Design and implement PostgreSQL schema for agreements, deposits, settlements, receipts, and webhooks tables. Set up Prisma/TypeORM with migrations

subtasks.title: Design PostgreSQL Database Schema
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive database schema design for all required tables including agreements, deposits, settlements, receipts, and webhooks with proper relationships and constraints
subtasks.details: Design tables with primary keys, foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Define relationships between agreements and deposits, settlements and receipts. Include fields for timestamps, status tracking, transaction IDs, and hash signatures. Consider data types for Solana addresses, USDC amounts, and NFT metadata.

subtasks.title: Initialize Prisma/TypeORM Configuration
subtasks.description: Set up ORM configuration files, database connection settings, and project structure for database operations
subtasks.details: Install and configure Prisma or TypeORM with PostgreSQL driver. Set up database connection string, environment variables, and configuration files. Create project structure for models, migrations, and database utilities.

subtasks.title: Create Database Models and Entities
subtasks.description: Implement ORM models/entities for agreements, deposits, settlements, receipts, and webhooks tables based on the designed schema
subtasks.details: Create model classes with proper field types, decorators, and relationships. Define enums for status fields, validation rules, and default values. Implement model methods for common operations and data transformations.

subtasks.title: Generate and Execute Initial Migration
subtasks.description: Create initial database migration files to set up all tables with proper structure, indexes, and constraints in PostgreSQL
subtasks.details: Generate migration files using ORM tools to create all tables with correct column types, primary keys, foreign keys, and indexes. Include proper constraints for data integrity and performance optimization. Execute migration against development database.

subtasks.title: Setup Migration Management System
subtasks.description: Configure migration tracking, versioning, and deployment processes for database schema changes across environments
subtasks.details: Set up migration tracking table, configure migration commands for different environments, create scripts for automated deployment. Document migration procedures and establish rollback strategies for production deployments.

tasks.title: Implement Agreement Creation API
tasks.description: Create POST /v1/agreements endpoint that accepts nft_mint, price, seller, optional buyer, expiry, fee_bps, honor_royalties. Calls on-chain program to init escrow PDA and returns agreement_id with deposit addresses

subtasks.title: Design API endpoint structure and request/response schemas
subtasks.description: Define the POST /v1/agreements endpoint structure with request validation schemas and response format
subtasks.details: Create OpenAPI specification for POST /v1/agreements endpoint. Define request schema with required fields (nft_mint, price, seller, expiry, fee_bps, honor_royalties) and optional buyer field. Define response schema with agreement_id and deposit addresses. Include proper HTTP status codes and error responses.

subtasks.title: Implement request validation middleware
subtasks.description: Create middleware to validate incoming request parameters and data types
subtasks.details: Implement validation for nft_mint (valid Solana public key), price (positive number), seller (valid public key), buyer (optional valid public key), expiry (future timestamp), fee_bps (0-10000 range), honor_royalties (boolean). Return appropriate error messages for invalid inputs.

subtasks.title: Integrate on-chain program escrow PDA initialization
subtasks.description: Implement service layer to call on-chain program for escrow PDA creation
subtasks.details: Create service function to interact with Solana on-chain program. Generate escrow PDA using agreement parameters. Call init_escrow instruction with validated parameters. Handle transaction confirmation and error cases. Extract PDA address and deposit addresses from transaction result.

subtasks.title: Implement database storage for agreement records
subtasks.description: Create database schema and service to store agreement data with generated agreement_id
subtasks.details: Design database table for agreements with fields: agreement_id (UUID), nft_mint, price, seller, buyer, expiry, fee_bps, honor_royalties, escrow_pda, deposit_addresses, created_at, status. Implement database service with create agreement function. Generate unique agreement_id and store all parameters.

subtasks.title: Implement complete API endpoint handler
subtasks.description: Create the final POST /v1/agreements endpoint handler that orchestrates all components
subtasks.details: Implement Express.js route handler that combines validation, on-chain interaction, and database storage. Handle success response with agreement_id and deposit addresses. Implement proper error handling with rollback mechanisms. Add logging for monitoring and debugging. Return structured JSON response matching API specification.

tasks.title: Implement Solana Blockchain Monitoring Service
tasks.description: Create service to monitor escrow vault accounts for USDC and NFT deposits. Validate NFT mint, metadata, and amount. Update database with deposit status

subtasks.title: Set up Solana RPC connection and account monitoring infrastructure
subtasks.description: Establish connection to Solana RPC nodes and implement account subscription mechanism for monitoring escrow vault accounts
subtasks.details: Configure Solana web3.js client with RPC endpoints, implement WebSocket connections for real-time account monitoring, set up connection pooling and failover mechanisms for reliability

subtasks.title: Implement USDC deposit detection and validation
subtasks.description: Create service to detect USDC token transfers to escrow vault accounts and validate deposit amounts
subtasks.details: Monitor SPL token account changes for USDC mint, parse transaction logs to extract transfer amounts, validate against expected deposit amounts from database, handle decimal precision correctly

subtasks.title: Implement NFT deposit detection and metadata validation
subtasks.description: Create service to detect NFT transfers to escrow accounts and validate NFT mint addresses and metadata
subtasks.details: Monitor NFT token account changes, fetch and validate NFT metadata from on-chain and off-chain sources, verify mint address matches expected NFT, validate collection membership if required

subtasks.title: Implement database update service for deposit status
subtasks.description: Create service to update database records with deposit confirmation status and transaction details
subtasks.details: Update escrow agreement records with deposit status, store transaction signatures and block confirmations, implement atomic database operations to prevent race conditions, add logging for audit trail

subtasks.title: Implement monitoring service orchestration and error handling
subtasks.description: Create main monitoring service that coordinates deposit detection, handles errors, and manages service lifecycle
subtasks.details: Implement service startup/shutdown procedures, add comprehensive error handling and retry logic, implement health checks and monitoring metrics, add configuration management for RPC endpoints and monitoring intervals

tasks.title: Implement Atomic Settlement Engine
tasks.description: Create service that detects when both NFT and USDC are locked, validates not expired, calls on-chain settle instruction to swap assets, calculates and deducts platform fee, optionally honors creator royalties

subtasks.title: Implement Lock Detection Service
subtasks.description: Create service to monitor and detect when both NFT and USDC assets are locked in escrow
subtasks.details: Build a monitoring service that polls blockchain state to detect when both parties have locked their assets (NFT and USDC) in the escrow contract. Include event listeners for lock confirmations and maintain state tracking.

subtasks.title: Implement Expiration Validation
subtasks.description: Create validation logic to check if escrow agreements have not expired before settlement
subtasks.details: Implement timestamp validation against current block time to ensure the escrow agreement is still within its valid time window. Include buffer time considerations and proper error handling for expired agreements.

subtasks.title: Implement On-Chain Settlement Caller
subtasks.description: Create service to call the on-chain settle instruction to execute the asset swap
subtasks.details: Build the interface to interact with the blockchain settlement contract, including proper transaction construction, signing, and submission. Handle transaction failures and implement retry logic with exponential backoff.

subtasks.title: Implement Platform Fee Calculator
subtasks.description: Create fee calculation and deduction system for platform fees from settlements
subtasks.details: Implement configurable fee calculation logic that computes platform fees as percentage of transaction value. Include fee deduction from settlement amounts and proper accounting for fee collection wallet.

subtasks.title: Implement Creator Royalty Handler
subtasks.description: Create optional creator royalty calculation and distribution system
subtasks.details: Build system to detect NFT creator royalty requirements from metadata, calculate royalty amounts, and distribute payments to creators during settlement. Make royalty handling configurable and optional per agreement.

tasks.title: Implement Expiry and Cancellation Logic
tasks.description: Create service to check expiry timestamps, allow refunds for partial deposits, implement admin cancel with multisig, update agreement status accordingly

subtasks.title: Create Expiry Timestamp Checking Service
subtasks.description: Implement a service that continuously monitors and checks expiry timestamps for active escrow agreements
subtasks.details: Create a background service that periodically queries the database for active agreements and checks their expiry timestamps against current time. Service should identify expired agreements and trigger appropriate actions.

subtasks.title: Implement Partial Deposit Refund Logic
subtasks.description: Create functionality to calculate and process refunds for agreements with partial deposits when cancellation occurs
subtasks.details: Implement logic to determine refund amounts based on current deposit status, handle USDC transfers back to depositors, and update agreement balances accordingly. Include validation for refund eligibility.

subtasks.title: Implement Admin Multisig Cancellation
subtasks.description: Create admin cancellation functionality that requires multisig approval for cancelling active agreements
subtasks.details: Implement multisig workflow for admin-initiated cancellations, including proposal creation, signature collection, and execution. Ensure proper authorization checks and audit logging for all admin actions.

subtasks.title: Implement Agreement Status Update Engine
subtasks.description: Create service to automatically update agreement status based on expiry checks and cancellation events
subtasks.details: Implement status transition logic that updates agreement status from active to expired, cancelled, or refunded based on various triggers. Include database updates and event emission for status changes.

subtasks.title: Integrate Expiry and Cancellation Services
subtasks.description: Integrate all expiry and cancellation components into a cohesive system with proper error handling and monitoring
subtasks.details: Combine all services into a unified expiry and cancellation system, implement comprehensive error handling, add monitoring and alerting for failed operations, and ensure proper cleanup of resources.

tasks.title: Implement Agreement Query and Cancel Endpoints
tasks.description: Create GET /v1/agreements/{id} to return status, balances, deadlines. Create POST /v1/agreements/{id}/cancel to trigger cancellation for expired agreements

subtasks.title: Design Agreement Data Model and Database Schema
subtasks.description: Define the database schema and data structures for storing agreement information including status, balances, and deadlines
subtasks.details: Create database tables/collections to store agreement data with fields for agreement_id, status (active/expired/cancelled), buyer_balance, seller_balance, nft_locked_status, usdc_locked_status, expiry_deadline, created_at, updated_at. Define TypeScript interfaces and validation schemas.

subtasks.title: Implement Agreement Repository Layer
subtasks.description: Create database access layer with methods to query and update agreement records
subtasks.details: Implement repository class with methods: findAgreementById(), updateAgreementStatus(), getAgreementBalances(), checkExpiryStatus(). Include proper error handling for not found cases and database connection issues.

subtasks.title: Create GET /v1/agreements/{id} Endpoint
subtasks.description: Implement REST endpoint to retrieve agreement details including status, balances, and deadlines
subtasks.details: Create Express.js route handler that validates agreement ID parameter, calls repository to fetch agreement data, formats response with status, buyer/seller balances, NFT/USDC lock status, expiry deadline. Return 404 for non-existent agreements.

subtasks.title: Implement Agreement Expiry Detection Logic
subtasks.description: Create service to detect expired agreements and validate cancellation eligibility
subtasks.details: Implement service methods to check if agreement has passed expiry deadline, validate that agreement is in cancellable state (not already settled/cancelled), and determine if cancellation is authorized. Include timezone handling for deadline comparisons.

subtasks.title: Create POST /v1/agreements/{id}/cancel Endpoint
subtasks.description: Implement REST endpoint to trigger cancellation of expired agreements
subtasks.details: Create Express.js route handler that validates agreement ID, checks expiry status using detection service, updates agreement status to cancelled, triggers any necessary cleanup operations (unlock assets, notify parties). Return appropriate HTTP status codes and error messages.

tasks.title: Implement Webhook System
tasks.description: Create webhook service with events (escrow.funded, escrow.asset_locked, escrow.settled, escrow.expired, escrow.refunded). Implement retry logic, signature verification, and configurable webhook URLs

subtasks.title: Design webhook event system architecture
subtasks.description: Design the webhook event system to handle escrow lifecycle events with proper event definitions and payload structures
subtasks.details: Define event types (escrow.funded, escrow.asset_locked, escrow.settled, escrow.expired, escrow.refunded), create event payload schemas with relevant data fields like agreement ID, timestamps, amounts, and transaction hashes. Design event publishing interface and webhook configuration storage.

subtasks.title: Implement webhook URL management and configuration
subtasks.description: Create system to manage and configure webhook URLs for different events with validation and storage
subtasks.details: Build webhook URL registration API endpoints, implement URL validation (format, reachability), create database schema for storing webhook configurations per user/organization, add CRUD operations for webhook management with proper authentication.

subtasks.title: Implement signature verification system
subtasks.description: Create cryptographic signature system to verify webhook authenticity and prevent tampering
subtasks.details: Implement HMAC-SHA256 signature generation using secret keys, add signature headers to webhook requests, create signature verification utilities for webhook consumers, manage webhook secrets securely with rotation capabilities.

subtasks.title: Build webhook delivery service with retry logic
subtasks.description: Create reliable webhook delivery system with exponential backoff retry mechanism and failure handling
subtasks.details: Implement webhook HTTP client with timeout configuration, build exponential backoff retry logic (max 5 retries), add dead letter queue for failed deliveries, implement delivery status tracking and logging, create webhook delivery queue system.

subtasks.title: Integrate webhook system with escrow lifecycle events
subtasks.description: Connect webhook system to escrow state changes to automatically trigger webhook deliveries
subtasks.details: Add webhook triggers to escrow funding, asset locking, settlement, expiration, and refund processes. Implement event publishing from escrow state machines, ensure webhook delivery doesn't block main escrow operations using async processing, add webhook delivery monitoring and alerting.

tasks.title: Implement Settlement Receipt Generation
tasks.description: Create service to generate hash-signed settlement receipts with nft_mint, price, fees, timestamps, transaction IDs. Store in database and make available via API

subtasks.title: Design Settlement Receipt Data Structure
subtasks.description: Define the complete data structure for settlement receipts including all required fields and validation rules
subtasks.details: Create TypeScript interfaces/types for settlement receipt containing nft_mint, price, fees breakdown, timestamps (created, settled), transaction IDs (escrow, settlement), buyer/seller addresses, agreement ID, and hash signature field. Define validation schemas for each field including format requirements and constraints.

subtasks.title: Implement Hash Signing Service
subtasks.description: Create cryptographic service to generate secure hash signatures for settlement receipts
subtasks.details: Implement hash signing functionality using SHA-256 or similar algorithm to create tamper-proof signatures for receipt data. Include private key management for signing and public key verification. Create utility functions to generate deterministic hashes from receipt data and sign with server private key.

subtasks.title: Create Database Schema and Models
subtasks.description: Design and implement database table structure for storing settlement receipts with proper indexing
subtasks.details: Create settlement_receipts table with columns for all receipt fields including indexed fields like agreement_id, nft_mint, buyer_address, seller_address, created_at. Implement database model/entity classes with proper relationships to agreements table. Add database migrations and ensure proper constraints and foreign keys.

subtasks.title: Build Receipt Generation Service
subtasks.description: Implement core service logic to generate settlement receipts from completed escrow transactions
subtasks.details: Create SettlementReceiptService class that takes settlement transaction data, populates receipt structure, generates hash signature, and stores in database. Include methods to extract transaction details, calculate fee breakdowns, format timestamps, and handle error cases. Integrate with atomic settlement engine to trigger receipt generation.

subtasks.title: Implement Receipt API Endpoints
subtasks.description: Create REST API endpoints to retrieve settlement receipts with proper authentication and filtering
subtasks.details: Implement GET /v1/receipts/{id} and GET /v1/receipts endpoints with query parameters for filtering by agreement_id, nft_mint, date range, buyer/seller addresses. Add proper authentication, input validation, pagination, and response formatting. Include receipt verification endpoint to validate hash signatures.

tasks.title: Setup Redis for Caching and Queues
tasks.description: Configure Redis client, implement caching for agreement lookups, set up job queues for blockchain monitoring and settlement processing

subtasks.title: Install and Configure Redis Client
subtasks.description: Set up Redis client library and establish connection configuration for the application
subtasks.details: Install redis-py library, create Redis connection class with connection pooling, configure connection parameters (host, port, password, SSL), implement connection health checks and retry logic

subtasks.title: Implement Agreement Caching System
subtasks.description: Create caching layer for agreement data to improve lookup performance and reduce database load
subtasks.details: Design cache key structure for agreements, implement cache-aside pattern with TTL configuration, create methods for get/set/delete operations, handle cache invalidation on agreement updates

subtasks.title: Setup Job Queue Infrastructure
subtasks.description: Configure Redis-based job queue system for asynchronous task processing
subtasks.details: Implement job queue using Redis lists or streams, create job serialization/deserialization, add job priority handling, implement dead letter queue for failed jobs, configure job retry mechanisms

subtasks.title: Implement Blockchain Monitoring Queue
subtasks.description: Create specialized job queue for blockchain event monitoring and processing tasks
subtasks.details: Design job structure for blockchain monitoring tasks, implement periodic blockchain scanning jobs, create event processing workflows, add block confirmation tracking, handle blockchain reorganization scenarios

subtasks.title: Setup Settlement Processing Queue
subtasks.description: Implement job queue system for handling escrow settlement and payment processing tasks
subtasks.details: Create settlement job types (release funds, refund, dispute resolution), implement job scheduling for time-based settlements, add transaction validation workflows, integrate with payment processing systems

tasks.title: Implement Security Middleware
tasks.description: Add rate limiting, CORS configuration for easyescrow.ai domain, input validation, USDC mint allowlist, authentication for protected endpoints

subtasks.title: Implement Rate Limiting Middleware
subtasks.description: Create rate limiting middleware to prevent API abuse and ensure fair usage across all endpoints
subtasks.details: Implement rate limiting using a sliding window or token bucket algorithm. Configure different limits for different endpoint types (e.g., 100 requests/minute for general endpoints, 10 requests/minute for settlement operations). Store rate limit counters in Redis or in-memory cache. Return appropriate HTTP 429 status codes when limits are exceeded.

subtasks.title: Configure CORS for easyescrow.ai Domain
subtasks.description: Set up Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) configuration to allow requests from easyescrow.ai domain
subtasks.details: Configure CORS middleware to allow requests from easyescrow.ai and its subdomains. Set appropriate allowed methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), headers (Content-Type, Authorization, X-API-Key), and credentials handling. Ensure preflight requests are handled correctly for complex requests.

subtasks.title: Implement Input Validation Middleware
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive input validation middleware to sanitize and validate all incoming request data
subtasks.details: Implement validation for all API endpoints using schema validation (e.g., Joi, Yup, or similar). Validate data types, required fields, string lengths, numeric ranges, and format constraints. Sanitize inputs to prevent injection attacks. Return detailed validation error messages with HTTP 400 status codes.

subtasks.title: Implement USDC Mint Allowlist Validation
subtasks.description: Create middleware to validate that only approved USDC mint addresses are accepted in transactions
subtasks.details: Maintain a configurable allowlist of approved USDC mint addresses. Create middleware that validates incoming requests containing USDC mint addresses against this allowlist. Support for updating the allowlist through admin endpoints or configuration. Reject transactions with non-approved mint addresses with appropriate error messages.

subtasks.title: Implement Authentication Middleware for Protected Endpoints
subtasks.description: Create authentication middleware to secure protected API endpoints and validate user credentials
subtasks.details: Implement JWT-based authentication middleware for protected endpoints. Validate JWT tokens, check expiration, and extract user information. Support for API key authentication for service-to-service communication. Implement role-based access control if needed. Return HTTP 401 for invalid tokens and HTTP 403 for insufficient permissions.

tasks.title: Create Docker Configuration
tasks.description: Create Dockerfile for containerized backend, optimize for production, configure environment variables, health check endpoint

subtasks.title: Create base Dockerfile with multi-stage build
subtasks.description: Create a Dockerfile with multi-stage build pattern to optimize image size and security for production deployment
subtasks.details: Set up multi-stage Dockerfile with builder stage for dependencies and lean production stage. Use official Node.js Alpine image as base, copy package files, install dependencies, and build application. Create final stage with minimal runtime dependencies.

subtasks.title: Configure environment variables and secrets management
subtasks.description: Set up environment variable configuration with proper defaults and secrets handling for containerized deployment
subtasks.details: Define environment variables for database connections, API keys, JWT secrets, and other configuration. Use Docker secrets or environment files for sensitive data. Set up proper variable validation and fallback values.

subtasks.title: Implement health check endpoint
subtasks.description: Create a dedicated health check endpoint that validates application and database connectivity for container orchestration
subtasks.details: Implement /health endpoint that checks database connectivity, external service availability, and application readiness. Return appropriate HTTP status codes and health information. Configure endpoint to be lightweight and fast.

subtasks.title: Optimize Docker image for production
subtasks.description: Apply production optimizations including security hardening, performance tuning, and minimal attack surface
subtasks.details: Remove development dependencies, set non-root user, configure proper file permissions, minimize installed packages. Add security scanning, optimize layer caching, and configure proper logging. Set resource limits and startup parameters.

subtasks.title: Configure Docker health check and startup probes
subtasks.description: Set up Docker HEALTHCHECK instruction and configure container orchestration probes for reliable deployments
subtasks.details: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction to Dockerfile using the health endpoint. Configure appropriate intervals, timeouts, and retry counts. Document probe configuration for Kubernetes readiness and liveness probes.

tasks.title: Setup DigitalOcean App Platform Deployment
tasks.description: Configure App Platform deployment from GitHub, add managed PostgreSQL and Redis via VPC, configure secrets, set up api.easyescrow.ai domain with HTTPS, enable health checks and autoscaling

subtasks.title: Configure GitHub Repository Connection
subtasks.description: Connect DigitalOcean App Platform to GitHub repository for automated deployments
subtasks.details: Set up GitHub integration in DigitalOcean App Platform, configure repository access, set up branch-based deployment triggers, and configure build settings for the backend application

subtasks.title: Create Managed PostgreSQL Database
subtasks.description: Set up managed PostgreSQL database instance with VPC configuration
subtasks.details: Create PostgreSQL database cluster in DigitalOcean, configure VPC networking for secure access, set up database credentials, and configure connection pooling and backup settings

subtasks.title: Create Managed Redis Instance
subtasks.description: Set up managed Redis instance for caching and job queues with VPC access
subtasks.details: Create Redis cluster in DigitalOcean, configure VPC networking to match PostgreSQL setup, set up Redis authentication, and configure memory limits and eviction policies

subtasks.title: Configure App Secrets and Environment Variables
subtasks.description: Set up all required environment variables and secrets for the application
subtasks.details: Configure database connection strings, Redis URLs, JWT secrets, blockchain RPC endpoints, and other sensitive configuration in App Platform's encrypted environment variables

subtasks.title: Setup Custom Domain with HTTPS and Health Checks
subtasks.description: Configure api.easyescrow.ai domain with SSL certificate and enable monitoring
subtasks.details: Add custom domain api.easyescrow.ai to App Platform, configure automatic SSL certificate, set up health check endpoints, configure autoscaling rules based on CPU/memory usage, and test domain resolution

tasks.title: Write Comprehensive Tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive test suite including on-chain Rust/Anchor tests for smart contract functionality, off-chain API integration tests, unit tests for all services, and detailed test matrix covering happy path scenarios, edge cases, security vulnerabilities, and system invariants
tasks.details: Implement a complete testing strategy covering both on-chain smart contract testing using Rust/Anchor with local validator, and off-chain API testing for integration scenarios. Include detailed test matrix for settlement flows, security edge cases, fee calculations, authorization checks, and system reliability patterns.
tasks.testStrategy: Multi-layered testing approach: on-chain tests using Rust/Anchor framework with local Solana validator for smart contract logic, integration tests for API endpoints with real database interactions, unit tests with mocking for isolated component testing, and comprehensive edge case coverage including security attack vectors

subtasks.title: Create Unit Tests for Core Services
subtasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for all core services including payment processing, user management, and business logic components
subtasks.details: Implement unit tests using a testing framework like Jest or Mocha. Cover all public methods, mock external dependencies, test error handling, and achieve high code coverage for services developed in tasks 24, 26, 27, 29, 30

subtasks.title: Develop Integration Tests for API Endpoints
subtasks.description: Create integration tests for all REST API endpoints to verify end-to-end functionality and proper request/response handling
subtasks.details: Set up test database, create test fixtures, write integration tests for all API endpoints from task 28. Test authentication, authorization, request validation, and response formats using tools like Supertest or similar

subtasks.title: Implement On-Chain Smart Contract Tests
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive Rust/Anchor tests for all smart contract functionality using local Solana validator environment
subtasks.details: Set up local Solana validator testing environment and implement comprehensive on-chain tests covering: happy path settlements (USDC first then NFT, and vice versa), wrong mint rejection (invalid USDC or NFT mints), under/over-funding protection (price-1, price+1 scenarios), double-settle and double-deposit no-ops, expiry refund functionality (only depositor withdrawal), precise fee collection and optional royalties distribution, unauthorized cancel blocking with admin-cancel multisig requirements, PDA/account spoofing prevention with account metadata permutation tests, rent-exempt vault management with proper rent release on closing, and event field validation matching state for receipt generation

subtasks.title: Implement Off-Chain API Integration Tests
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive integration tests for off-chain API functionality focusing on reliability, idempotency, and data consistency
subtasks.details: Implement integration tests covering: deterministic agreement_id generation and validation, idempotency key handling for duplicate request prevention, exact-once webhook delivery guarantees, RPC failure retry logic with exponential backoff, partial success recovery mechanisms, and database invariant enforcement (no negative balances, monotonic status transitions only)

subtasks.title: Implement Happy Path Test Scenarios
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive test cases covering all successful user journeys and normal system operations
subtasks.details: Write test scenarios for successful payment flows, user registration, deposit processing, settlement completion, and normal cancellation processes. Ensure all positive paths work correctly from start to finish including both on-chain and off-chain components

subtasks.title: Test Edge Cases and Security Scenarios
subtasks.description: Develop comprehensive tests for edge cases, security vulnerabilities, and attack vector prevention
subtasks.details: Create specific test cases for: early cancellation before deposit, late cancellation after settlement, partial deposit scenarios, double-settlement prevention, automatic expiry refunds, network failures, timeout handling, account spoofing attempts, unauthorized access patterns, and fee calculation precision to the lamport level

subtasks.title: Set Up Test Infrastructure and CI Integration
subtasks.description: Configure comprehensive test environment including local Solana validator, test databases, and automated CI/CD pipeline integration
subtasks.details: Set up local Solana validator for on-chain testing, configure test databases, implement CI/CD pipeline to run all test suites automatically, create test reporting and coverage analysis for both Rust and JavaScript/TypeScript code, develop test data seeding scripts, and establish comprehensive testing best practices documentation covering both on-chain and off-chain testing methodologies

tasks.title: End-to-End Devnet Testing
tasks.description: Perform comprehensive end-to-end testing on devnet with specific test scenarios: happy path (create → deposit USDC → deposit NFT → settle → receipt), expiry path (create → partial deposit → expire → refund), and concurrency testing (race condition handling for multiple buyers)
tasks.details: Execute three critical test scenarios on actual Solana devnet with real wallets to validate complete system functionality before mainnet deployment. Tests must cover the full transaction lifecycle, error handling, and edge cases including race conditions.
tasks.testStrategy: Run tests on actual Solana devnet using real wallets and transactions. Validate blockchain state changes, transaction confirmations, and system behavior under various conditions including network delays and concurrent operations.

subtasks.title: Setup Devnet Testing Environment
subtasks.description: Configure and prepare the devnet environment for comprehensive end-to-end testing
subtasks.details: Set up devnet RPC endpoints, configure wallet with sufficient SOL for transaction fees, prepare test USDC tokens and NFT collections, verify Solana program deployment on devnet, configure environment variables for testing

subtasks.title: Execute Happy Path End-to-End Test
subtasks.description: Test the complete happy path scenario: create agreement → deposit USDC → deposit NFT → atomic settle → receipt JSON published
subtasks.details: Create new escrow agreement with test parameters, perform USDC deposit from buyer wallet, deposit NFT from seller wallet, execute atomic settlement, verify receipt JSON is published with complete transaction details. Validate all state transitions and final balances.

subtasks.title: Execute Expiry Path Test Scenario
subtasks.description: Test expiry and refund functionality: create agreement → deposit one side only → wait for expiry → execute refund
subtasks.details: Create escrow agreement, deposit either USDC or NFT (but not both), allow agreement to expire, execute expiry refund instruction, verify correct refund to original depositor and proper cleanup of escrow account.

subtasks.title: Execute Concurrency Race Condition Test
subtasks.description: Test race condition handling when multiple buyers attempt to fund the same open offer simultaneously
subtasks.details: Set up scenario with one open offer and two buyer wallets attempting to deposit USDC simultaneously. Execute concurrent transactions and verify only one succeeds while the other fails gracefully. Test system's ability to handle race conditions properly.

subtasks.title: Validate Fee Collection and Receipt Generation
subtasks.description: Test fee collection mechanism and receipt validation for completed transactions across all test scenarios

Localnet provides fast, free, deterministic testing environment for TDD of all state machine logic.
tasks.details: This localnet setup serves as the critical foundation for comprehensive test-driven development of the escrow program's state machine. The local validator environment enables rapid iteration through all test scenarios without devnet costs or network delays, supporting deterministic testing of complex edge cases and error conditions that are essential for robust smart contract development.
tasks.testStrategy: Validate localnet setup by running a subset of the complete test matrix to ensure all testing infrastructure is properly configured. Test should include basic program deployment, account creation, token operations, and state transitions to confirm the environment supports the full range of planned test scenarios.

tasks.title: Implement Idempotency Keys for Critical Endpoints

subtasks.details: Create database table with fields: idempotency_key (unique), endpoint_path, request_hash, response_status, response_body, created_at, expires_at. Define TTL for key expiration (24-48 hours). Design indexes for efficient lookups.

subtasks.title: Implement Idempotency Key Validation Middleware

subtasks.details: Extract Idempotency-Key header, validate format (UUID v4), check for required endpoints (/v1/agreements, /v1/agreements/{id}/settle). Return 400 for missing/invalid keys on protected endpoints.

subtasks.title: Implement Duplicate Request Detection Logic
subtasks.description: Build service to detect duplicate requests by comparing idempotency keys and request content hashes
subtasks.details: Generate SHA-256 hash of request body and parameters. Check database for existing idempotency key. If found with same hash, return cached response. If found with different hash, return 409 Conflict.

subtasks.details: Wrap agreement creation logic with idempotency checks. Store successful responses with 201 status. Handle race conditions with database constraints. Ensure atomic operations between agreement creation and idempotency key storage.

tasks.title: Setup Production Database Infrastructure on DigitalOcean
tasks.description: Implement simple, safe MVP production database layout using DO managed services: Managed Postgres cluster with three logical databases (prod/stage/dev), Managed Redis for idempotency and queues, DO Spaces for receipts, with proper security, PITR backups, connection pooling, and least-privilege roles
tasks.details:
**Backend API:** DO App Platform (`api.easyescrow.ai`)

**Database:** DO Managed Postgres in same region with three logical DBs in one cluster:
- `easyescrow_prod` (real users)
- `easyescrow_stage` (staging/smoke on devnet)
- `easyescrow_dev` (playground)

**Cache/Queues:** DO Managed Redis for idempotency keys, webhooks, retries

**Storage:** DO Spaces (S3-compatible) for receipts/artifacts

**Key Requirements:**
- Enable private networking between App Platform and managed services
- Turn on PITR (point-in-time recovery) + daily snapshots
- Create least-privilege roles: `app_user_prod` (R/W, no DDL), `migrate_user_prod` (DDL only), equivalent for stage/dev
- Enable PgBouncer connection pooling
- Configure migration pipeline gated behind `migrate_user_*` roles
- Store secrets via App Platform env vars with quarterly rotation policy
tasks.testStrategy: Validate database setup with connection tests from App Platform, verify role permissions, test PITR recovery, validate migration process in stage environment before prod

tasks.title: Migrate from Upstash to Redis Cloud for Bull Queue Compatibility
tasks.description: Migrate Redis infrastructure from Upstash to Redis Cloud (redis.io) due to Bull job queue incompatibility with Upstash's Lua script limitations. Redis Cloud provides full Redis compatibility required for Bull's dynamic key generation in Lua scripts.
tasks.details:
## Problem
- Bull job queues are fundamentally incompatible with Upstash Redis
- Upstash requires all Lua script keys as explicit arguments, but Bull generates dynamic keys inside scripts
- Current error rate: 16.5% (EPIPE/ECONNRESET)
- Deployments failing at 4/6 (runtime errors, no logs)
- Jobs processing unreliably

## Solution
Migrate to Redis Cloud Free Tier (redis.io):
- Full Bull/BullMQ compatibility
- 30GB storage, 30 connections (free forever)
- AWS Singapore region (ap-southeast-1) for low latency to DO apps
- IP allowlist + TLS for cross-provider security
- AOF persistence for data reliability

## Expected Results
- 0% error rate (down from 16.5%)
- Successful deployments (6/6 phases)
- Reliable Bull queue processing
- All 3 Bull connections working (client, subscriber, bclient)

## Security Requirements
- TLS encryption (rediss:// protocol)
- IP allowlist configured for DigitalOcean app IPs
- Strong password authentication
- Regular password rotation (monthly)

## Environments
- **Dev**: Free tier (30GB) - easyescrow-dev
- **Staging**: Free tier or Fixed 250MB ($5/mo) - easyescrow-staging
- **Production**: Fixed 1GB ($12/mo) with HA - easyescrow-prod

subtasks.details: Sign up for Redis Cloud account at redis.io, create new subscription with Free tier (30GB storage, 30 connections), select AWS ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) region for low latency to DigitalOcean apps, name database 'easyescrow-dev', configure basic settings and obtain connection details

subtasks.testStrategy: Test connection with TLS from allowed IP and verify rejection from non-allowlisted IP

subtasks.testStrategy: Run comprehensive Bull queue test suite with job creation, processing, and completion verification

subtasks.testStrategy: Monitor application logs for 24 hours, verify 0% error rate, test all Bull queue operations, confirm successful deployment phases

tasks.title: E2E Devnet Test: NFT-to-USDC Swap with Fee Collection
tasks.description: Create end-to-end test on Solana devnet for NFT swap with USDC payment and fee collection. Test the complete flow of escrow service with 3 wallets: sender (NFT owner), receiver (USDC payer), and fee collector (1% USDC fee).
tasks.details:
## Test Scenario
Happy path test for NFT-to-USDC swap on devnet with 3 wallets:
- **Sender Wallet**: Owns NFT, receives USDC payment (minus fee)
- **Receiver Wallet**: Pays USDC, receives NFT
- **Fee Collector Wallet**: Receives 1% fee in USDC

## Prerequisites & Setup

### 1. Wallet Setup
- Verify 3 test wallets exist and are funded with SOL (for transaction fees)
- Check existing setup in `scripts/fund-devnet-wallets.ps1`
- Small values: Use ~0.1 USDC for swap, 0.001 USDC for fee

### 2. USDC Token Setup
**Challenge**: Wallets funded with SOL need USDC tokens
**Solutions**:
a) Use Solana Devnet USDC faucet (if available)
b) Mint devnet USDC tokens using spl-token CLI:
   - Create USDC token mint (or use existing devnet USDC mint)
   - Mint tokens to receiver wallet (~0.2 USDC for testing)
   - Mint tokens to fee collector for initial setup verification
c) Use Solana devnet faucet to airdrop devnet USDC

**Recommended**: Use SPL Token CLI to mint test USDC tokens on devnet

### 3. NFT Creation
**Challenge**: Create NFT in sender's wallet
**Solutions**:
a) Use Metaplex CLI to create test NFT collection and mint
b) Use @metaplex-foundation/js SDK programmatically:
   ```typescript
   import { Metaplex } from '@metaplex-foundation/js';
   const metaplex = Metaplex.make(connection);
   const nft = await metaplex.nfts().create({ ... });
   ```
c) Leverage existing `scripts/setup-nft-collection.ps1` if available

**Recommended**: Use Metaplex JS SDK within test setup to programmatically create NFT

## Test Implementation Steps

### Phase 1: Test Setup
1. Load/verify 3 devnet wallets (sender, receiver, fee-collector)
2. Ensure all wallets have SOL for transaction fees (~0.1 SOL each)
3. Create/mint test NFT in sender wallet using Metaplex
4. Mint devnet USDC tokens to receiver wallet (0.2 USDC)
5. Verify initial balances

### Phase 2: Create Escrow
1. Sender creates escrow transaction via API
2. Deposits NFT into escrow program
3. Specifies USDC amount (0.1 USDC) and receiver wallet
4. System calculates 1% fee (0.001 USDC)
5. Verify escrow state in on-chain program

### Phase 3: Execute Swap
1. Receiver accepts escrow via API
2. Transfers USDC (0.1 USDC) to escrow
3. System distributes:
   - 99% (0.099 USDC) to sender
   - 1% (0.001 USDC) to fee collector
4. NFT transferred to receiver
5. Escrow marked as completed

### Phase 4: Verification
1. Verify NFT ownership changed to receiver
2. Verify sender received 0.099 USDC
3. Verify fee collector received 0.001 USDC
4. Verify escrow status is COMPLETED
5. Verify all transactions on Solana explorer

## Technical Considerations

### Token Setup Commands (Reference)
```bash
# Create USDC mint on devnet (or use existing)
spl-token create-token --decimals 6

# Create token account for receiver
spl-token create-account <MINT_ADDRESS>

# Mint USDC to receiver
spl-token mint <MINT_ADDRESS> 0.2 <RECEIVER_TOKEN_ACCOUNT>
```

### NFT Creation (Reference)
```typescript
const nft = await metaplex.nfts().create({
  uri: 'https://arweave.net/mock-metadata',
  name: 'Test NFT for E2E',
  sellerFeeBasisPoints: 0,
  creators: [{ address: senderWallet.publicKey, share: 100 }],
});
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- `tests/e2e/devnet-nft-usdc-swap.test.ts` - Main test file
- `tests/helpers/devnet-token-setup.ts` - Helper for USDC minting
- `tests/helpers/devnet-nft-setup.ts` - Helper for NFT creation
- `tests/fixtures/devnet-wallets.ts` - Wallet management

## Success Criteria
✅ All 3 wallets set up with SOL
✅ Test NFT created in sender wallet
✅ Receiver wallet has USDC tokens
✅ Escrow created successfully on-chain
✅ NFT transferred to receiver
✅ USDC payment distributed correctly (99% to sender, 1% to fee collector)
✅ All balances verified post-transaction
✅ Test runs reliably on devnet
✅ Transaction costs stay under 0.05 SOL total

## Scope
**Included**: Happy path only - successful swap with all conditions met
**Excluded**: Error paths, cancellations, timeouts (future tasks)

tasks.title: Generate new Solana program keypair
tasks.description: Generate a fresh keypair for the escrow program deployment to ensure no ID conflicts
tasks.details:
Execute: solana-keygen new -o target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json --force

This will create a new keypair file that will be used to derive the program's address. The --force flag will overwrite any existing keypair.

After generation:
- Capture the new public key (program ID)
- Verify the keypair file exists at target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json
- Store the program ID for use in subsequent tasks

Expected output: A new keypair file and a base58-encoded public key

tasks.title: Update declare_id in Rust source code
tasks.description: Update the program ID in the Rust source code to match the newly generated keypair
tasks.details:
File to modify: programs/escrow/src/lib.rs (line 5)

Change:
declare_id!("7dVEyFFeMzAT3oUpyvXwchGfPQDuXHdQv5tyfDBztKuV");

To:
declare_id!("<NEW_PROGRAM_ID>");

Where <NEW_PROGRAM_ID> is the public key from the generated keypair.

This ensures the compiled program has the correct ID embedded in the bytecode.

Verification:
- Grep for declare_id to confirm the update
- Ensure the ID matches: solana address -k target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json

tasks.title: Update Anchor.toml with new program ID
tasks.description: Update the devnet program address in Anchor.toml configuration
tasks.details:
File to modify: Anchor.toml (line 9)

Change:
[programs.devnet]
escrow = "7dVEyFFeMzAT3oUpyvXwchGfPQDuXHdQv5tyfDBztKuV"

To:
[programs.devnet]
escrow = "<NEW_PROGRAM_ID>"

This tells Anchor which program ID to use for devnet deployments.

Verification:
- Read Anchor.toml and verify the ID matches the keypair
- Ensure the ID is consistent with the declare_id in lib.rs

tasks.title: Build Anchor program with matching IDs
tasks.description: Compile the Solana program with the updated program ID embedded in the bytecode
tasks.details:
Execute: anchor build

This will:
1. Compile the Rust program with the updated declare_id
2. Generate the program binary (.so file)
3. Create/update the IDL JSON file
4. Place artifacts in target/ directory

Expected outputs:
- target/deploy/escrow.so (compiled program)
- target/idl/escrow.json (Interface Definition Language file)
- target/types/escrow.ts (TypeScript types)

Verification:
- Check build completes without errors
- Verify .so file exists and has recent timestamp
- Confirm IDL has the correct program ID in "address" field

Timeout: Use 60s timeout wrapper for anchor build

tasks.title: Deploy program to Solana devnet
tasks.description: Deploy the compiled escrow program to Solana devnet
tasks.details:
Execute: anchor deploy --provider.cluster devnet

Prerequisites:
- Ensure wallet has sufficient SOL for deployment (check balance)
- Confirm RPC connection to devnet is working
- Verify anchor build completed successfully

The deployment will:
1. Upload the program binary to devnet
2. Create the program account at the keypair's address
3. Set the program as executable
4. Initialize upgrade authority (if applicable)

Expected result:
- Program deployed at the new program ID
- Transaction signature returned
- Program shows as deployed: solana program show <PROGRAM_ID> --url devnet

Error handling:
- If insufficient funds: airdrop SOL to wallet
- If RPC timeout: retry with longer timeout
- If program already exists: use upgrade instead

Timeout: Use 180s timeout wrapper for deployment

tasks.title: Update backend environment variables
tasks.description: Update all environment configurations with the new program ID
tasks.details:
Files to update:

1. nodemon.json (line 9):
   "ESCROW_PROGRAM_ID": "<NEW_PROGRAM_ID>"

2. docker-compose.yml (if ESCROW_PROGRAM_ID is defined there)

3. .env file (if exists)

4. Any other config files that reference the old program ID

Optional but recommended:
- Update tests/fixtures/devnet-config.json if it has program references
- Update docs that mention the program ID
- Update README.md with new deployment info

Verification:
- Grep for old program ID to find all references
- Ensure consistency across all config files
- No hardcoded old IDs remain in source code

tasks.title: Copy new IDL to backend source
tasks.description: Update the backend's IDL file with the newly generated one from anchor build
tasks.details:
Execute: Copy target/idl/escrow.json to src/generated/anchor/escrow-idl.json

This ensures the backend TypeScript code uses the correct program interface.

Steps:
1. Verify target/idl/escrow.json has the new program ID
2. Backup existing src/generated/anchor/escrow-idl.json (optional)
3. Copy the new IDL file
4. Verify the copied file has correct program ID in "address" field

The IDL contains:
- Program address
- Instruction definitions
- Account structures
- Error codes
- Type definitions

Backend service (EscrowProgramService) will use this to construct transactions.

tasks.title: Restart Docker services
tasks.description: Gracefully restart backend services to load new configuration
tasks.details:
Execute: docker compose restart backend

This will:
1. Stop the backend container gracefully
2. Reload environment variables from nodemon.json
3. Restart with new ESCROW_PROGRAM_ID

Alternative (if full rebuild needed):
docker compose up -d --build backend

Verification after restart:
- Check container is healthy: docker compose ps
- View logs: docker compose logs -f backend
- Look for "EscrowProgramService initialized with program: <NEW_PROGRAM_ID>"
- Confirm no program ID mismatch errors

Health check:
curl http://localhost:3000/api/health

Should return status 200 with no errors about program configuration.

tasks.title: Verify program deployment and backend integration
tasks.description: Run comprehensive verification tests to ensure program and backend are working correctly
tasks.details:
Verification checklist:

1. Verify on-chain program:
   solana program show <NEW_PROGRAM_ID> --url devnet
   - Should show program exists
   - Check program size
   - Verify upgrade authority

2. Verify IDL matches:
   anchor idl fetch <NEW_PROGRAM_ID> --provider.cluster devnet
   - Compare with src/generated/anchor/escrow-idl.json
   - Ensure instructions match

3. Test backend health:
   curl http://localhost:3000/api/health
   - Should return 200 OK
   - No program configuration errors

4. Check backend logs:
   docker compose logs backend | Select-String "EscrowProgramService"
   - Look for initialization success
   - Confirm program ID matches
   - No mismatch errors

5. Test program interaction (optional):
   - Create test escrow agreement
   - Verify PDA derivation works
   - Check transaction succeeds

Expected result: All verifications pass, no ID mismatch errors, program is functional

tasks.title: Pre-flight: Verify Program ID and Keypair Match
tasks.description: Verify that the intended Program ID matches the keypair at target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json and restore from backup if there's a mismatch.
tasks.details:
This pre-flight verification ensures the program keypair is correct before deployment:

1. Check current keypair address:
   ```bash
   solana address -k target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json
   ```

2. Verify the output matches the expected Program ID: 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd

3. If there's a mismatch, restore from backup:
   ```bash
   cp temp/escrow-keypair-4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd.json target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json
   ```

4. Re-verify after restoration:
   ```bash
   solana address -k target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json
   ```

This verification prevents deployment issues caused by incorrect keypairs and ensures consistency with the expected program configuration. The backup keypair should have been created during the initial keypair generation process.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Execute `solana address -k target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json` and confirm output is exactly: 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd
2. If mismatch occurs, verify backup file exists at temp/escrow-keypair-4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd.json
3. After restoration (if needed), re-run address command and verify correct output
4. Check file permissions and ensure keypair file is readable by deployment tools
5. Document any mismatch found and restoration performed for audit trail

tasks.testStrategy:
1. **File verification**: Use grep to search for the program ID in each of the four target files:

tasks.title: Clean Build and Deploy Fresh Program to Devnet
tasks.description: Perform a clean build of the escrow program with unified program ID and deploy fresh artifacts to Solana devnet using the pinned program keypair.
tasks.details:
Execute a complete clean build and deployment process to ensure all artifacts are regenerated with the correct unified program ID:

**Step 1: Clean Build**
```bash
anchor build
# OR alternatively: cargo build-sbf
```
This regenerates all artifacts including:
- IDL files with correct program ID
- Compiled program binary (.so file)
- TypeScript types
- All metadata with unified program ID

**Step 2: Deploy with Pinned Keypair**
```bash
solana program deploy \
  --url devnet \
  --program-id target/deploy/escrow-keypair.json \
  target/deploy/escrow.so
```

**Step 3: Verify Deployment**
- Confirm transaction signature is returned
- Verify program is executable on devnet
- Check program account exists at expected address: 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd

**Important Considerations:**
- Ensure wallet has sufficient SOL for deployment fees
- Verify devnet RPC connection is stable
- Clean build ensures no stale artifacts with old program IDs
- Using --program-id flag ensures deployment to specific address

**Expected Outputs:**
- Fresh program binary deployed to devnet
- Transaction signature confirming successful deployment
- Program account active and executable
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Build Verification**: Confirm `anchor build` completes without errors and generates fresh artifacts with recent timestamps in target/deploy/
2. **Deployment Success**: Verify deployment command returns a transaction signature and completes without errors
3. **Program Address Verification**: Execute `solana program show --url devnet 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd` to confirm program exists and is executable
4. **IDL Consistency Check**: Verify the generated IDL file contains the correct program address matching the deployed program ID
5. **Transaction Confirmation**: Check the deployment transaction on Solana Explorer (devnet) to confirm successful program deployment
6. **Functional Test**: Execute a simple program instruction call to verify the deployed program is operational and responding correctly

tasks.title: Propagate New IDL to Backend and Restart Services
tasks.description: Sync the updated IDL to the application and restart services to pick up changes with the new program ID.
tasks.details:
This task ensures the backend application uses the freshly built IDL with the correct program ID after a clean deployment:

**Step 1: Copy Fresh IDL**
```bash
cp target/idl/escrow.json src/generated/anchor/escrow-idl.json
```
This overwrites the backend's IDL file with the newly generated one from `anchor build`, ensuring the TypeScript code uses the correct program interface and address.

**Step 2: Restart Backend Services**
```bash
docker compose restart backend
```
This gracefully restarts the backend container to:
- Reload environment variables from nodemon.json
- Pick up the new IDL file
- Initialize services with the updated program ID

**Step 3: Verify Backend Startup**
Monitor the startup logs to confirm:
- Container restarts successfully
- EscrowProgramService initializes with correct program ID
- No program ID mismatch errors occur
- All services are healthy and responding

**Step 4: Health Check**
Verify the backend is operational:
- Check container status: `docker compose ps`
- View logs: `docker compose logs -f backend`
- Test API endpoints are responding
- Confirm program ID matches in service initialization logs

This task completes the deployment pipeline by ensuring the backend application layer is synchronized with the freshly deployed Solana program.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **IDL File Verification**: Confirm `src/generated/anchor/escrow-idl.json` has been updated by checking the "address" field matches the deployed program ID: 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd

2. **Container Restart Success**: Verify `docker compose restart backend` completes without errors and container shows "healthy" status in `docker compose ps`

3. **Startup Log Verification**: Check backend logs contain the message "EscrowProgramService initialized with program: 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd" and no program ID mismatch errors

4. **Service Health Check**: Test that backend API endpoints respond correctly and services are fully operational after restart

5. **End-to-End Verification**: Confirm the backend can successfully interact with the deployed Solana program using the updated IDL

tasks.title: Verify Program ID Alignment Before E2E Tests
tasks.description: Run comprehensive verification checks to catch any lingering program ID mismatches before executing end-to-end tests.
tasks.details:
This task performs a final verification sweep to ensure all components are aligned with the correct program ID (4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd) before running e2e tests:

**Step 1: Verify On-Chain Program**
```bash
solana program show 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd --url devnet
```
Confirms the program exists on devnet and is executable.

**Step 2: Verify IDL Matches Deployed Program**
```bash
anchor idl fetch 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd --provider.cluster devnet
```
Fetches the on-chain IDL and compares it with local artifacts to ensure consistency.

**Step 3: Check Backend Environment Variable**
```bash
echo $ESCROW_PROGRAM_ID
```
Verifies the backend service has the correct program ID configured (should output: 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd).

**Step 4: Verify Test Fixtures Configuration**
Check that test configuration files point to devnet with the correct program ID:
- Verify test environment variables
- Confirm test RPC endpoints use devnet
- Validate test fixtures use the correct program address

**Step 5: Cross-Reference All Components**
Ensure consistency across:
- Local IDL file (src/generated/anchor/escrow-idl.json)
- On-chain program
- Backend configuration
- Test setup

This verification prevents e2e test failures due to program ID mismatches and ensures all components are properly synchronized.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **On-Chain Program Verification**: Execute `solana program show 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd --url devnet` and verify:
   - Command returns successfully (exit code 0)

2. **IDL Consistency Check**: Run `anchor idl fetch 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd --provider.cluster devnet` and verify:
   - Command completes without errors
   - Fetched IDL structure matches local src/generated/anchor/escrow-idl.json
   - Address field in both IDLs shows 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd

3. **Backend Environment Verification**: Execute `echo $ESCROW_PROGRAM_ID` and confirm output is exactly: 4FQ5JoxsS5jjuTR1ScuEpk66eX5B71L7ysJEysmsTwhd

4. **Test Configuration Validation**: 
   - Grep test files for program ID references
   - Verify test RPC endpoints point to devnet
   - Confirm no hardcoded references to old program IDs

5. **Health Check**: Run `curl http://localhost:3000/api/health` to ensure backend service starts without program configuration errors

Success criteria: All verification steps pass with consistent program ID across all components.
